response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile full-stack engineer with genuine distributed-systems exposure (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic) and informal leadership through founding GISCollective and running workshops. However, this role's core minimum qualification is 8 years of C++, and the resume shows only about six months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis. The primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no production cloud-compute-platform or large-scale infrastructure work in C++. The hard requirements are not met.
strengths: Distributed-systems experience with high-throughput, low-latency microservices
Broad engineering breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps/CI-CD
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and founding a project
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum (~6 months of C/C++ experience)
No production cloud architecture or internal compute-platform experience
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; current career gap
